title: Brain manifest
description: Per-project brain stamp. Records identity, the framework version the brain runs, enabled platforms and core modules, project-owned extensions, and the app repos the brain coordinates. The manifest is the boundary between hub-owned core (upgradeable via down-sync) and project-owned extensions (never overwritten).

manifest_version.description: Schema version of this manifest file, for migration across framework versions.

slug.description: Lowercase identifier used in repo/db/service names (e.g. "acme").

framework_version.description: The hub framework version this brain was last synced to. Compared against brain-factory/registry/framework-version.json during the down-sync (the upgrade command).

command_prefix.description: Per-brain command prefix. Commands are invoked as <command_prefix>-<base> (e.g. prefix 'wg' gives 'wg-sync'). The framework does not hardcode a prefix; the donor Northwind system uses 'xs', each project picks its own short prefix at provision time. The engine stamps this into core skill/prompt names ({{CMD_PREFIX}}).

platforms.description: Which adapter runtimes are installed for this brain. Extensible: additional runtimes may be added without changing callers.

agent_runtimes.description: Which AI agent runtimes this brain emits command artifacts for and can operate under. Open and swappable: no single vendor is required. 'none' means the brain's deterministic adapter tasks run with no LLM at all, which every brain must support. Paid/enterprise runtimes (e.g. multi-agent orchestration) are additive and never required for core operation. Extensible: more runtimes may be added without changing callers.

core_modules.description: Hub-owned modules enabled for this brain. Upgraded by the down-sync. Keys are core module ids; values record the enabled state and the framework version that last touched them.

synced_from.description: Framework version that last applied this module.

adopted.description: true if installed by the hub; false if a pre-existing project artifact the inspect-first onboarding chose to keep.

extensions.description: Project-owned capabilities. Never overwritten by the down-sync. Each entry names a project-specific command/skill or policy living under the brain's extensions namespace.

app_repos.description: App/code repos this brain coordinates. A brain may coordinate one or many.

onboarding.description: How this brain was created, for audit.

gap_report.description: Path to the inspect-first gap report, if adopt-existing.
